# XN-001 — Plugin vs no-plugin on deepseek-v3.2

*Generated by research-copilot.*

## TL;DR

At model parity (deepseek-v3.2) on the canonical 36-task easy-setting eval,
the `repo3` plugin (geos-rag skill + 3-DB ChromaDB MCP) **wins by +0.175
mean TreeSim (0.828 vs 0.653) and +34 percentage points of >=7/10 pass rate
(88.6% vs 54.3%) on the paired 35 tasks scored in both runs**. This resolves
the main gap flagged in `misc/ablation_findings.md` and gives the paper its
first clean positive adaptation result.

Caveat: single seed per condition. Cross-seed variance is unmeasured.

## Conditions

| Run | Agent | Harness | Model | Timeout | Seeds |
|---|---|---|---|---|:---:|
| `ablation_deepseek_v2` (E01) | `claude_code_no_plugin` | `claude_native` in container, NO plugin, NO MCP | deepseek-v3.2 | 1200s | 1 |
| `repo3_eval_run4` (E03) | `claude_code_repo3_plugin` | `claude_native` in container, plugin enabled, GEOS-RAG MCP on per-task ChromaDB copy | deepseek-v3.2 | 1200s | 1 |

Both share:
- 36-task set (identical list, derived from `t1_cc_deepseek_clean2`)
- `run/AGENTS.md` system prompt + GEOS primer injected via `--append-system-prompt`
- Docker container per task, filtered GEOS mount, contamination blocklist
- `run/Dockerfile` image
- Ground truth: `/data/shared/geophysics_agent_data/data/eval/experiments_gt/`

Only difference: plugin vs no plugin.

Numbers cross-checked against raw `*_eval.json` files under
`/data/shared/geophysics_agent_data/data/eval/results/<run>/<agent>/`.
No mock-data flags set. Plugin run had 1 task (`ExampleSPE11b`)
**timeout at 1200s** leaving a partial XML that the scorer could not
parse; excluded from paired stats. E01 scored SPE11b at 0.287.
Including SPE11b as 0 in E03 and 0.287 in E01 would change the paired
mean comparison from 0.828 vs 0.653 to 0.805 vs 0.645 on the full 36
tasks — sign and approximate magnitude preserved.

**Retry asymmetry (single task):** E01 `TutorialSneddon` triggered the
runner's `failed_no_outputs` retry (`attempt_1` + main `status.json`
both archived on disk). First attempt: 352.6s with one `AskUserQuestion`
call and no XML. Retry: 683.9s with a modified task prompt noting the
prior failure, producing XML scored 0.099. E03 had zero retried tasks.
So for Sneddon specifically, E01 got slightly more compute budget and a
modified prompt; E03 got a single shot. This imperfectly affects the
+0.705 Sneddon delta. Sensitivity check: removing Sneddon from both
runs, paired mean stays ~0.827 vs ~0.669 on 34 tasks; sign and ~0.16
delta preserved. Plugin win is not Sneddon-dependent.

## Aggregate (paired 35 tasks)

| Metric | plugin+ds (E03) | no-plugin+ds (E01) | delta |
|---|---:|---:|---:|
| Mean TreeSim | **0.828** | 0.653 | **+0.175** |
| Median TreeSim | 0.852 | 0.708 | +0.144 |
| Range | [0.537, 0.998] | [0.099, 0.954] | — |
| Pass >=0.7 (count) | **31/35 (88.6%)** | 19/35 (54.3%) | **+12 tasks** |
| Wins (>plugin) | **29** | 6 | — |
| Ties | 0 | 0 | — |

Non-paired note: E01 also scored `ExampleSPE11b` at 0.287; E03 failed to
parse E03's `ExampleSPE11b` output and was excluded. Including it in E03
as 0 would still leave E03 at mean 0.805 — plugin still decisively wins.

## Biggest plugin wins (top 8)

| Delta | E03 plugin | E01 no-plug | Task |
|---:|---:|---:|---|
| +0.705 | 0.804 | 0.099 | TutorialSneddon |
| +0.686 | 0.992 | 0.305 | ExampleDPWellbore |
| +0.673 | 0.948 | 0.275 | ExampleMandel |
| +0.657 | 0.880 | 0.224 | AdvancedExampleCasedElasticWellboreImperfectInterfaces |
| +0.560 | 0.804 | 0.244 | TutorialDeadOilEgg |
| +0.345 | 0.998 | 0.653 | AdvancedExampleViscoModifiedCamClay |
| +0.304 | 0.847 | 0.542 | AdvancedExampleCasedContactThermoElasticWellbore |
| +0.303 | 0.918 | 0.615 | AdvancedExampleModifiedCamClay |

The pattern: the plugin rescues tasks where the no-plugin baseline produced
essentially empty/wrong XML (scores <0.3). RAG-assisted discovery of the
right example / schema appears to matter most when the agent would
otherwise flail.

## Plugin losses (all 6 cases below parity)

| Delta | E03 plugin | E01 no-plug | Task |
|---:|---:|---:|---|
| -0.171 | 0.537 | 0.708 | AdvancedExampleWellboreNonLinearThermalDiffusionTemperatureDependentSinglePhaseThermalConductivity |
| -0.151 | 0.756 | 0.907 | AdvancedExampleDeviatedPoroElasticWellbore |
| -0.116 | 0.740 | 0.856 | pknViscosityDominated |
| -0.107 | 0.596 | 0.704 | ExampleTFrac |
| -0.088 | 0.866 | 0.954 | pennyFracToughnessDominated |
| -0.039 | 0.898 | 0.937 | pennyFracViscosityDominated |

Losses are small (max -0.17). No single-task catastrophic regression.
Pattern hypothesis to test: RAG may occasionally return a misleading
example that anchors the agent to a near-but-wrong template (the poroelastic
/ fracture-mechanics tasks share a lot of surface structure). Worth
looking at per-task RAG retrievals to confirm.

## Honesty / limitations

- **Single seed per condition.** Don't know how much of the +0.175 is
  seed-dependent variance. Multiple seeds per condition is the next
  priority before calling this finding "locked."
- **Single model (deepseek-v3.2).** RQ3 (does the plugin win hold on other
  models?) is entirely open. Minimax result exists (`repo3_eval_run2` at
  0.809) but with no paired no-plugin run. Gemma / Opus untested.
- **Single task setting (easy).** If difficulty-tier work lands, the
  plugin's value proposition may change (harder setting may benefit more
  from RAG; or not).
- **Can't attribute the gain inside the plugin.** The plugin bundles the
  `geos-rag` skill prompt/instructions and the 3-DB ChromaDB MCP server.
  A skill-only vs MCP-only decomposition is needed to say *what* helps.
  Tracked as I11 in the DAG.
- **SPE11b timeout (not parse error) in the plugin run.** The agent ran
  out the 1200s wall while mid-write. Counts as one of 5 E03 timeouts
  (see "Timeout accounting" in hub.md State of Knowledge).
- **Misleading telemetry in E01 `status.json`:** every no-plugin task
  logs `plugin_tool_calls >= 1`, `rag_tool_calls >= 1`, and
  `rag_requirement_met: true`. Reading `events.jsonl` confirms the
  underlying tool calls returned
  `tool_use_error: No such tool available: mcp__geos-rag__...` —
  the MCP tools were NOT wired in no-plugin and no data was returned.
  The counter increments on rejected tool calls, not successful ones.
  No contamination occurred; but this will mislead any reader opening
  a raw status file. Fix the logging in a follow-up.
- **Cost / wall-clock not compared.** The plugin run's cost stats were not
  captured by the collaborator; need to sum from `events.jsonl` if a cost
  claim is needed.

## Artifacts

- E03 per-task evals: `/data/shared/geophysics_agent_data/data/eval/results/repo3_eval_run4/claude_code_repo3_plugin/*_eval.json`
- E01 per-task evals: `/data/shared/geophysics_agent_data/data/eval/results/ablation_deepseek_v2/claude_code_no_plugin/*_eval.json`
- E03 raw task dirs: `/data/shared/geophysics_agent_data/data/eval/claude_code_repo3_plugin/repo3_eval_run4/`
- E01 raw task dirs: `/home/matt/sci/repo3/data/eval/claude_code_no_plugin/ablation_deepseek_v2/`
- Scoring log: `misc/score_run4.log`
- Paired comparison: `misc/e03_vs_e01.txt`
- Compare script: `misc/compare_runs.py`

## Implications for next experiments

1. **Report-worthy for the advisor meeting.** This is the first concrete
   "our adaptation beats vanilla CC at model parity" result.
2. **Stacking question (I10):** does adding a memory/cheatsheet ON TOP of
   the plugin yield further gains? If yes, paper story gets richer.
3. **Attribution question (I11):** skill-only vs MCP-only plugin runs
   would tell us whether to invest in better RAG or better skill prompts.
4. **Robustness:** run 2 more seeds of both conditions to put a std on
   +0.175. Non-blocking for meeting; required before paper.
